title: ACI Tenant Configuration Compliance Audit
exclusions: Per-change **aaaModLR** intent, immediacy, and blast-radius storytelling (**UC-18.1.5**); fabric-wide numeric health dials without tenant object trees (**UC-18.1.1**); **actrlRuleHit5min** permit/deny ratios (**UC-18.1.9**); **vzBrCP** counter-only analytics (**UC-18.1.4**); physical uplink saturation (**UC-18.1.6**); multi-vendor SDN configuration journals outside **APIC** MO parity (**UC-18.3.8**); **Nexus 9000** standalone **NX-OS** fabrics (**UC-18.4.x**).
evidence: Saved search **`aci_uc1816_tenant_compliance_gaps`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** with primary **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:tenant_config`** plus companion panels for **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:health`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, and **`cisco:aci:syslog`** freshness; Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_tenant_compliance.json`** exposing **`tok_tenant`**/**`tok_fab`** drill tokens and **lookup** **`tenant_compliance_exceptions`** overlays.
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) merging five **TA**-aligned sourcetypes for completeness work: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:tenant_config`** (scheduled **`GET /api/class/fvTenant.json?rsp-subtree=full`** plus flatteners for **`fvAp`**, **`fvBD`**, **`fvCtx`**, **`vzAny`**, **monitoring**, **SNMP**, **syslog** descendants exposing **`tenant_name`**, **`vzAny_mode`**, **`monEPGPol_dn`**, **`snmp_trap_dest_count`**, **`syslog_remote_dest_count`**, **`ctx_with_unicast_routing`**, **`bd_routing_consistent`**, **`application_profile_with_epg`**); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**`aaaModLR`**, **`configJob`**) for change correlation; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (**Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, Splunkbase **4022**) for mnemonic maintenance and fault strings; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** for **faultInst** codes that often accompany half-built tenants; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** for collector/soak context—poll cadence **60–600s**, volume proportional to tenant counts and subtree depth.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022), package **`Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`**) for **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:health`** parsing; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**`rest_ta`**, [Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or hardened Python collectors authenticated via **`aaaLogin.json`**/**`aaaRefresh.json`** for **MO** pulls into **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`**.
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:tenant_config" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval tenant=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_name,tenant,tn,fvTenant_name,tenant_code,"unknown")))
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)uni/tn-(?<tn_from_dn>[^/\s\"]+)"
| eval tenant=if(tenant="unknown" AND isnotnull(tn_from_dn),lower(tn_from_dn),tenant)
| eval fab=lower(trim(coalesce(fabric_name,pod_name,site_code,"aci")))
| eval vz_ok=if(tonumber(vzany_restricted_flag)=1 OR tonumber(restrict_vzany)=1 OR tonumber(vzAny_has_contracts_only)=1 OR match(lower(coalesce(vzAny_mode,vzany_mode,vzany_contract_stance,"")),"deny|restrict|consumed|provided"),1,0)
| eval mon_ok=if(tonumber(mon_epg_pol_linked)=1 OR tonumber(monFabricAttached)=1 OR isnotnull(monEPGPol_dn) OR isnotnull(monFabricPol_dn),1,0)
| eval snmp_ok=if(tonumber(snmp_trap_dest_count)>0 OR tonumber(snmpDestCount)>0 OR match(lower(_raw),"snmptrapdest|snmpgroup"),1,0)
| eval syslog_ok=if(tonumber(syslog_remote_dest_count)>0 OR tonumber(syslogDestCount)>0 OR match(lower(_raw),"syslogremotedest|sysloggroup"),1,0)
| eval vrf_ok=if(tonumber(ctx_with_unicast_routing)=1 OR tonumber(ctxUniRouteYes)=1,1,0)
| eval bd_ok=if(tonumber(bd_routing_consistent)=1 OR tonumber(bdL3Consistent)=1 OR bd_arpflood_ok=1,1,0)
| eval ap_ok=if(tonumber(application_profile_with_epg)=1 OR tonumber(epgTotal)>0 OR tonumber(epg_count_under_tenant)>0,1,0)
| eval name_ok=if(match(tenant,"^(prd|dev|tst|sha|dmz|corp|nc|lab|shared|sec)_[a-z0-9_-]{3,56}$"),1,0)
| eval checks_pass=vz_ok+mon_ok+snmp_ok+syslog_ok+vrf_ok+bd_ok+ap_ok+name_ok
| eval checks_total=8
| eval missing=checks_total-checks_pass
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*checks_pass/checks_total,2)
| eval ingest_skew=if(isnotnull(_indextime) AND isnotnull(_time),_indextime-_time,null())
| eval raw_v=if(vz_ok=0,"vzAnyRestriction|","") . if(mon_ok=0,"MonitoringPolicy|","") . if(snmp_ok=0,"SnmpExport|","") . if(syslog_ok=0,"SyslogExport|","") . if(vrf_ok=0,"VrfUnicastRouting|","") . if(bd_ok=0,"BridgeDomainRouting|","") . if(ap_ok=0,"ApplicationProfileEpg|","") . if(name_ok=0,"NamingConvention|","")
| eval violation_detail=replace(replace(replace(raw_v,"^\\|+",""),"\\|+$",""),"\\|"," | ")
| where missing>0
| eval priority=case(missing>=5,"sev_high",missing>=3,"sev_med",1=1,"sev_low")
| eventstats median(ingest_skew) as skew_p50 by fab
| eval collector_lag_flag=if(isnotnull(ingest_skew) AND isnotnull(skew_p50) AND ingest_skew>(skew_p50*5) AND ingest_skew>600,1,0)
| sort 0 - missing - compliance_pct tenant
| head 400
| table tenant fab priority compliance_pct missing violation_detail vz_ok mon_ok snmp_ok syslog_ok vrf_ok bd_ok ap_ok name_ok ingest_skew collector_lag_flag
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as lt values(Change.object) as changed_dn count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where nodename=Change.All_Changes AND (All_Changes.object="*uni/tn-*" OR All_Changes.object="*fvTenant*" OR All_Changes.object="*vzAny*") by Change.user Change.action Change.src
| append [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as inv_t values(Inventory.device_id) as inv_id values(Inventory.category) as inv_cat count from datamodel=Inventory.All_Inventory where nodename=Inventory.All_Inventory by Inventory.vendor_product Inventory.dest]
| sort - lt
| head 300
schema: CIM **Change** ingests **aaaModLR** and export metadata so completeness gaps pair with the last mutating actor; **Inventory** carries slower-moving **fvTenant** and appliance identifiers once **FIELDALIAS** maps **object**/**dest** fields for CMDB joins.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Change** after **FIELDALIAS** maps **user**, **object**, **action**, **src** from **`cisco:aci:audit`** lines; accelerate **Inventory** only when normalized **device_id**/**vendor_product** fields are stable—retain raw **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`** JSON for attorney-ready snapshots.
description: Scores each **Cisco ACI** **`fvTenant`** subtree for enterprise **baseline completeness**: **vzAny** contract restrictions (rejecting permit-all shortcuts), **monEPGPol**/**monFabricPol** monitoring attachments, **snmpGroup**/**snmpTrapDest** export reachability, **syslogGroup**/**syslogRemoteDest** fan-out, **fvCtx** **unicastRouting**, **fvBD** routing/**ARP** consistency, **fvAp**/**fvAEPg** presence, and **`tn-{name}`** naming patterns—surfacing **missing=** counts and **`violation_detail`** strings distinct from **UC-18.1.5** timelines that explain **who** edited **aaaModLR** rows.
value: Architecture and **GRC** reviewers gain a defensible, automated **policy completeness** posture without opening every **APIC** pane before audits—silent gaps in **SNMP** or **syslog** exports starve downstream **SOC** correlation, loose **vzAny** pairings widen lateral movement risk, and absent **monitoring** policies stretch **MTTR** because faults lack required **EPG** instrumentation. Scoring **`compliance_pct`** per tenant turns tribal spreadsheet checks into scheduled evidence useful for **internal controls**, **SOC2**, and **CAB** readiness reviews.
implementation: Land **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** **4022**) for **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:health`**; schedule **HTTPS** collectors (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase** **1546**) for **`fvTenant`**, **`vzAny`**, **`monEPGPol`**, **`snmpGroup`**, **`syslogGroup`** into **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`**. Codify **`FIELDALIAS`**, **`tenant_compliance_exceptions`** lookups, macro **`m_aci_tenant_baseline`**, and dashboard tokens **`tok_tenant`**/**`tok_fab`**. Pair alerts with **`aci_uc1815_tenant_audit_risk`** for change context.
visualization: (1) **Heatmap** — **`fab`** × **`missing`** facet counts. (2) **Single-value** — median **`compliance_pct`** for production tenants. (3) **Table** — **`tenant`**, **`priority`**, **`violation_detail`**, per-check columns. (4) **Bar** — top offenders by **`missing`**. (5) **Drilldown** — opens **UC-18.1.5** change velocity panel filtered on same **`tenant`** plus **APIC** deep link.

references.title: Cisco APIC REST API Configuration Guide — Policy and Monitoring (Release 6.0(x))
Cisco APIC Managed Object Model Reference — fvTenant
Cisco APIC Configuration Best Practices — Tenant and vzAny Hardening

knownFalsePositives:
**greenfield_tenant_shells** — Automation provisions **`fvTenant`** placeholders hours before **`fvAp`**/**`fvBD`** arrive; **`ap_ok`** and **`bd_ok`** lag legitimately. Require **`lookup tenant_greenfield`**, **`CHG`**, or **`where _time`** gates that ignore shells younger than **SLA** hours.

**infrastructure_shared_tenants** — Shared services often waive per-tenant **SNMP** destinations because traps originate from **APIC** itself—encode **`allowed_gap_mask`** columns inside **`tenant_compliance_exceptions`** so **`snmp_ok`**/**`syslog_ok`** checks disable without mutating global SPL.

**l2_only_vrf_design** — Some **`fvCtx`** rows deliberately keep **`unicastRouting=no`** for pure bridging; **`vrf_ok`** fires falsely unless **`l2_only_flag=1`** travels from **CMDB** via **`lookup`**. Pair **`vrf_intent.csv`** refreshed nightly.

**mso_ndo_orchestration_lag** — **Multi-site** templates may delay **monEPGPol** attachment until after **`schema`** deploy completes—short **false positives** occur during **template push** windows. Correlate **`cisco:aci:audit`** **txId** bursts and widen alert suppression using **`mso_push_windows`** lookup keyed by **`fabric_name`**.

**parser_upgrade_field_rename** — **`monEPGPol_dn`** might rename to **`monEPGPolDN`** after **TA** releases, zeroing **`mon_ok`** for every tenant until **`props.conf`** **`FIELDALIAS`** refresh lands. Maintain regression tests comparing **APIC** `moquery` output to Splunk **`fieldsummary`** after upgrades.

**duplicate_collector_replay** — Two modular inputs ingesting the same **`fvTenant`** payload double counts **`missing`** if dedupe fails—dedupe with **`| dedup sha256(_raw)`** macro or sourcetype-specific **`collector_id`** prior to scoring.

**drill_lab_fabric_bypass** — Naming regex may reject **`tn-testbed01`** style tenants—extend **`match`** OR clauses or maintain **`lab_tenants.csv`** bypass list via **`inputlookup`**. Document each bypass quarterly.

**snapshot_only_export_gap** — Overnight **`configJob`** JSON may omit optional **`rsp-subtree`** sections while online **`GET /api/class/fvTenant.json?rsp-subtree=full`** shows full trees—prefer live pulls for authoritative completeness or tag snapshot rows with **`export_kind=snapshot`** and **`where export_kind!=snapshot`** for alerting.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• APIC clusters on 6.0(x), 6.1(x), or newer supported trains expose HTTPS 443/tcp to Splunk-connected collectors; provision read-only fabric automation personas with RBAC limited to MIT branches that cover fv, vz, mon, snmp, and syslog families so completeness pollers do not share passwords with interactive admins where **aaaRefresh.json** rotations collide.
• Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase 4022](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022), package **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**) where APIC syslog terminates—keep **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`**, and **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** distinct so parser upgrades remain reversible.
• Add **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (rest_ta, [Splunkbase 1546](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or a hardened Python modular collector that authenticates with **`POST /api/aaaLogin.json`** then **`POST /api/aaaRefresh.json`** before class pulls—stamp **`collector_id`** metadata wherever two inputs hit the same VIP.
• Expect 60–600s poll intervals for large `fvTenant` subtrees; APIC CPU climbs when **`rsp-subtree=full`** wraps an entire common tenant—prototype with **`query-target-filter`** predicates that isolate production prefixes before expanding universe-wide traversals.
• Capacity planning should assume multi-megabyte JSON for expansive **`uni/tn-{tenant}`** dumps; compress on the wire and dedicate **`index=cisco_aci`** (rename per governance) with 180–400d retention for configuration evidence packs.

Step 1 — Configure data collection (baseline object completeness)
• **Tenant export lane** — schedule **`GET https://<apic>/api/class/fvTenant.json?rsp-subtree=full`** (or **`.../api/class/fvTenant.json?rsp-subtree=children&rsp-subtree-class=fvAp,fvBD,fvCtx,vzAny,vzBrCP`**) into **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:tenant_config`**. Each event should carry **`fvTenant.name`**, Distinguished Names under **`uni/tn-{tenant}`**, child counts for `fvAp`, `fvBD`, `fvCtx`, `vzAny`, and any `monEPGPol`, **`monFabricPol`**, `snmpGroup`, `syslogGroup`, **`snmpTrapDest`**, **`syslogRemoteDest`** links reachable from the subtree. Hash each payload with **`sha256(_raw)`** to dedupe replays.
• vzAny lane — **`GET /api/class/vzAny.json`** at 120–300s supplies contract restriction posture when not embedded in the full tenant pull—use it to double-check `vzEntry` scope vs open-permit narratives without re-walking every tenant.
• **Monitoring policies** — **`GET /api/class/monEPGPol.json`** plus **`GET /api/class/monFabricPol.json`** (when fabric-level monitors apply) confirm `monEPGPol` attachments referenced by **`fvRsMonBrIfPol`** / **`fvRsMonEPgPol`** style relations.
• **SNMP export lane** — **`GET /api/class/snmpGroup.json`** with children **`snmpTrapDest`** to populate **`snmp_trap_dest_count`** style fields—without trap destinations, SOC hand-offs lose asynchronous fault evidence.
• **Syslog export lane** — **`GET /api/class/syslogGroup.json`** with **`syslogRemoteDest`** children to prove **`syslogRemoteDest`** coverage for Splunk or SIEM forwards mandated by runbooks.
• **Health coupling (optional)** — continue landing **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** from existing scorecard pollers (UC-18.1.1) so **`collector_lag_flag`** logic can differentiate missing objects from stalled collectors when `ingest_skew` widens without MO deletions.
• **Audit coupling** — keep **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (`aaaModLR`) flowing (UC-18.1.5) so operators can pivot from a gap to the last engineer or automation principal that touched the missing subtree.

Step 2 — Create the search, saved report, and alert
Schedule saved search **`aci_uc1816_tenant_compliance_gaps`** (cron **`15 * * * *`**) with **`earliest=-24h@m`**; throttle **`tenant`** + **`fabric`** for **45–90 minutes** after a firing.

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:tenant_config" earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| eval tenant=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_name,tenant,tn,fvTenant_name,tenant_code,"unknown")))
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)uni/tn-(?<tn_from_dn>[^/\s\"]+)"
| eval tenant=if(tenant="unknown" AND isnotnull(tn_from_dn),lower(tn_from_dn),tenant)
| eval fab=lower(trim(coalesce(fabric_name,pod_name,site_code,"aci")))
| eval vz_ok=if(tonumber(vzany_restricted_flag)=1 OR tonumber(restrict_vzany)=1 OR tonumber(vzAny_has_contracts_only)=1 OR match(lower(coalesce(vzAny_mode,vzany_mode,vzany_contract_stance,"")),"deny|restrict|consumed|provided"),1,0)
| eval mon_ok=if(tonumber(mon_epg_pol_linked)=1 OR tonumber(monFabricAttached)=1 OR isnotnull(monEPGPol_dn) OR isnotnull(monFabricPol_dn),1,0)
| eval snmp_ok=if(tonumber(snmp_trap_dest_count)>0 OR tonumber(snmpDestCount)>0 OR match(lower(_raw),"snmptrapdest|snmpgroup"),1,0)
| eval syslog_ok=if(tonumber(syslog_remote_dest_count)>0 OR tonumber(syslogDestCount)>0 OR match(lower(_raw),"syslogremotedest|sysloggroup"),1,0)
| eval vrf_ok=if(tonumber(ctx_with_unicast_routing)=1 OR tonumber(ctxUniRouteYes)=1,1,0)
| eval bd_ok=if(tonumber(bd_routing_consistent)=1 OR tonumber(bdL3Consistent)=1 OR bd_arpflood_ok=1,1,0)
| eval ap_ok=if(tonumber(application_profile_with_epg)=1 OR tonumber(epgTotal)>0 OR tonumber(epg_count_under_tenant)>0,1,0)
| eval name_ok=if(match(tenant,"^(prd|dev|tst|sha|dmz|corp|nc|lab|shared|sec)_[a-z0-9_-]{3,56}$"),1,0)
| eval checks_pass=vz_ok+mon_ok+snmp_ok+syslog_ok+vrf_ok+bd_ok+ap_ok+name_ok
| eval checks_total=8
| eval missing=checks_total-checks_pass
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*checks_pass/checks_total,2)
| eval ingest_skew=if(isnotnull(_indextime) AND isnotnull(_time),_indextime-_time,null())
| eval raw_v=if(vz_ok=0,"vzAnyRestriction|","") . if(mon_ok=0,"MonitoringPolicy|","") . if(snmp_ok=0,"SnmpExport|","") . if(syslog_ok=0,"SyslogExport|","") . if(vrf_ok=0,"VrfUnicastRouting|","") . if(bd_ok=0,"BridgeDomainRouting|","") . if(ap_ok=0,"ApplicationProfileEpg|","") . if(name_ok=0,"NamingConvention|","")
| eval violation_detail=replace(replace(replace(raw_v,"^\\|+",""),"\\|+$",""),"\\|"," | ")
| where missing>0
| eval priority=case(missing>=5,"sev_high",missing>=3,"sev_med",1=1,"sev_low")
| eventstats median(ingest_skew) as skew_p50 by fab
| eval collector_lag_flag=if(isnotnull(ingest_skew) AND isnotnull(skew_p50) AND ingest_skew>(skew_p50*5) AND ingest_skew>600,1,0)
| sort 0 - missing - compliance_pct tenant
| head 400
| table tenant fab priority compliance_pct missing violation_detail vz_ok mon_ok snmp_ok syslog_ok vrf_ok bd_ok ap_ok name_ok ingest_skew collector_lag_flag
```

Understanding this SPL
• Purpose: Unlike UC-18.1.5, which narrates who mutated aaaModLR rows, this pipeline scores **whether required MO families exist** inside each `fvTenant` snapshot—vzAny restriction posture, monitoring attachments, SNMP/syslog export paths, unicast VRF readiness, bridge-domain routing consistency, **application-profile** EPG presence, and `tn-{name}` naming.
• `vz_ok` prefers numeric **`vzany_restricted_flag`** from your normaliser; fallback patterns treat `deny`, `restrict`, `consumed`, `provided` stances as compliant because they imply contracts rather than permit-all openness—tighten with **`vzAny_has_contracts_only`** if your standard forbids ambiguous modes.
• `mon_ok` looks for monEPGPol/monFabricPol linkage—rename fields to match `FIELDALIAS` macros after TA upgrades.
• `snmp_ok`/`syslog_ok` admit either structured counts or regex hits on raw JSON when intermediate flatteners lag—prefer explicit counters for auditability.
• `fab` plus **`eventstats median(ingest_skew)`** highlights collectors lagging peers—use it before declaring a tenant “empty” when the issue is ingest backlog.
• Tune **`match(tenant,...)`** prefixes (`prd_`, `dev_`, `dmz_`, etc.) to your CMDB naming law; `common` and infrastructure tenants may need **`lookup` exceptions** (see **knownFalsePositives**).

Pipeline walkthrough (selected stages)
• Scopes **`index=cisco_aci`** **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:tenant_config`**—confirm nightly volume matches APIC tenant cardinality × poll frequency.
• Normalises `tenant` keys from multiple aliases and `rex`-extracts `tn-` fragments when flatteners omitted `tenant_name`.
• Derives eight binary checks, sums **`checks_pass`**, and computes **`compliance_pct`** for leadership-friendly tables.
• Builds human-readable **`violation_detail`** chains for PagerDuty narratives.
• Flags `priority` tiers based on `missing` counts so sev_high fires only when multiple control families drop at once.

Step 3 — Validate against APIC and architecture records
• In APIC, open **Tenants → {tenant} → Settings** and verify SNMP and syslog policies visually match Splunk `snmp_ok`/`syslog_ok`—drill into **`Fabric Policies → Monitoring Policies`** to confirm EPG monitoring attachments when `mon_ok` is 0.
• Compare **`vzAny` → subnets / contracts** view with Splunk’s `vz_ok` rationale; permit-all shortcuts usually expose `vzAny` entries lacking restrictive `vzSubj`/`vzBrCP` pairings.
• For `vrf_ok`, inspect **`Networking → VRFs`** ensuring **unicast routing** is enabled where L3Out or shared services require it—Splunk cannot infer intent if VRFs are deliberately L2-only; use **`tenant_compliance_exceptions`** for documented exceptions.
• For `bd_ok`, validate ARP flooding, **unicast routing**, and subnet alignment with your IPAM export—misaligned flags often mean parser drift, not true policy gaps.
• For `ap_ok`, ensure at least one **`Application Profiles`** node hosts `EPGs`—empty tenants mid-migration should align with CHG metadata instead of silent drift.
• Counts: `| timechart span=1h count by sourcetype` should never flatline for **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`** unless APIC maintenance windows pause collectors—correlate with **`cisco:aci:health`**.

Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards, access control, and runbooks
• **Executive row:** single-value median **`compliance_pct`** across production tenants plus top 10 `missing` counts—colour with **Dashboard Studio** thresholds (≥95 green, 85–94 amber, <85 red).
• **Operator row:** sortable table of `tenant`, `fab`, `priority`, **`violation_detail`**, per-check columns, **`ingest_skew`**, **`collector_lag_flag`** with drilldown tokens `tok_tenant`, `tok_fab`.
• **Pairing with audits:** embed a panel from **`aci_uc1815_tenant_audit_risk`** (UC-18.1.5) filtered to the same `tenant` so reviewers see **policy completeness** beside recent edits.
• Access: restrict indexes to **network-admins** and GRC roles; snmpGroup/syslogGroup details can reveal downstream collector IPs—mask if policy demands.
• **Alert payload:** include **`violation_detail`**, **`compliance_pct`**, deep link to APIC tenant, `CHG`, and last successful **`collector_id`** heartbeat.

Step 5 — Visualization, alert design, and troubleshooting
• **Visualization:** pair bar charts of `missing` facets (stacked `vz_ok`, `mon_ok`, etc.) with geomap or fabric icons keyed by `fab` when multi-site—add pie of `priority` for SLA reviews.
• **Alert design:** route `sev_high` to NOC + automation owners; `sev_med` to tenant stewards via lookup **`tenant_owner`**. Include **MITRE-friendly** wording only in ticketing tools—keep Splunk summaries vendor-specific.
• **Troubleshooting — no `cisco:aci:tenant_config` events:** verify rest_ta **`scheme/https`** targets, TLS trust stores, and RBAC denials logged in splunkd.log for the specific stanza name—re-run `aaaLogin` manually with curl to capture HTTP 401 bodies.
• **Troubleshooting — all checks show 1 but APIC disagrees:** flatteners stripped children—inspect `spath` output for missing **`snmpTrapDest`** arrays; widen **`rsp-subtree`** depth cautiously.
• **Troubleshooting — name_ok universally 0:** regex too strict—reopen prefixes with architecture guild; labs often use **`tn-demo`** patterns outside production rules.
• **Troubleshooting — ingest_skew spikes only on one VIP:** load balancer pinned a saturated APIC follower—rotate collectors or pin to leader per Cisco best practices for automation.
• **Troubleshooting — duplicate tenants:** merge streams from NDO and local APIC using **`fabric_name`** keys before `stats`—dedupe on **`sha256(_raw)`** fingerprints.

Anchors — fvTenant, vzAny, vzBrCP, monEPGPol, monFabricPol, snmpGroup, snmpTrapDest, syslogGroup, **syslogRemoteDest**, fvCtx, fvBD, fvAp, fvAEPg, **unicastRouting**, **`GET /api/class/fvTenant.json`**, **`GET /api/class/vzAny.json`**, **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, rest_ta, **`index=cisco_aci`**, **tenant_compliance_exceptions**.
grandmaExplanation: We line up each tenant against the standard checklist of required fabric pieces—monitoring hooks, trap routes, and naming rules—so empty rows mean blind spots even when recent edits look quiet.
hardware: Cisco APIC controllers (M3/M4/L4) fronting Nexus 9000 **ACI-mode** leaf/spine fabrics

controlTest.positiveScenario: A scheduled **`fvTenant`** export lands in **`cisco:aci:tenant_config`** where **`tenant=prd_payroll`** shows **`vz_ok=0`** because **`vzAny`** lacks restrictive contracts, **`snmp_ok=0`** because no **`snmpTrapDest`** children serialized, and **`mon_ok=0`** because **`monEPGPol`** links are absent—the alert **`aci_uc1816_tenant_compliance_gaps`** fires with **`missing>=3`** and lists the clauses inside **`violation_detail`**.
controlTest.negativeScenario: The same **`prd_payroll`** snapshot still shows gaps, but **`tenant_compliance_exceptions`** supplies **`allowed_gap_mask`** suppressing **SNMP**/**syslog** checks for infrastructure-style tenants and a **`maintenance_note`** referencing an approved **CHG**—after **`lookup`**, **`missing`** drops to **0** and the alert pipeline remains quiet while **UC-18.1.5** continues to show recent benign edits.
